workstream.name: ts2pant IR1 Capture-Avoiding Substitution
workstream.unlocks: Future workstreams that add IR1 substitution sites consume one well-tested primitive instead of rolling their own. Reduces the surface for capture bugs across the rest of the IR1 evolution. Specifically: `workstreams/ts2pant-l2-typed-mirror.md` M1 extends the primitive's binder-site enumeration with new forms (`comb-typed`, `forall`, `exists`, `comb`) as L1 grows to absorb the L2 vocabulary; general-loop SSA L5's break-substitution consumes the primitive instead of introducing a third hand-rolled walker.

problemStatement: ts2pant has two hand-rolled IR1 substitution walkers — `substituteL1Subtree` in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-build.ts` (functor-lift Member-operand rewriting, added M5 P4) and `substituteMemberReads` in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` (fixed-point lowering's Member-read replacement, added L4 P3). Both recurse over IR1 expressions and replace matching subtrees with a fresh-named replacement, relying on per-site fresh-binder allocation to avoid capture. PR #226's review surfaced this pattern as a recurring class of capture bugs: each new substitution site re-derives the hygiene discipline. The OCaml-backed `ast.substituteBinder` (Bindlib-backed, proven capture-avoiding) only substitutes Var-by-name on lowered `OpaqueExpr` — it cannot reach IR1 Member subtrees. So the per-site TS-side walkers stand in for it, each one a potential silent capture bug.
solutionSummary: Land one TS-side capture-avoiding substitution primitive (`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`) and migrate both existing hand-rolled walkers to use it, all in one atomic gameplan. The primitive enumerates IR1 binder sites exhaustively (`IR1Expr.each`, `IR1Stmt.let`, `IR1Stmt.foreach`, `IR1Stmt.for` init), throws `CaptureRiskError` when the replacement's free vars would be captured by a haystack binder, and respects Var-needle shadowing at the binder boundary. Tests combine hand-written unit cases per binder site with `fast-check`-driven property tests that generate random IR1 expressions with random binder structures. Migrations are snapshot-equivalent: every fixture exercising the old walkers produces byte-identical Pant output, gated by the existing constructs snapshot. Documentation lands a discipline statement: "all IR1 substitution goes through `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` / `substituteIR1StmtSubtree`; hand-rolled walkers in this layer are forbidden," with a PR #84-style post-mortem entry citing PR #226 as the IR1-layer instance.
currentStateAnalysis: Two existing walkers, both expression-level (operate on `IR1Expr`). `substituteL1Subtree` in `ir1-build.ts:1748` has partial binder-scope handling at the `each` case (stops descent for Var-needle name matches; doesn't handle Member needles or capture risk from the replacement). `substituteMemberReads` in `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts:358` has no binder-scope handling at all — it recurses into `each` body without checking the binder. Both work in production today because their callers carefully allocate replacements that are closed against scope (the functor-lift comprehension binder, the fixed-point state binder). The capture-avoidance discipline is correct but per-site; review consistently flags this as a concern (PR #226 the most recent). Beyond expressions, the IR1 vocabulary has binder-introducing statement forms (`IR1Stmt.let` name, `IR1Stmt.foreach` binder, `IR1Stmt.for` init's let) that no existing walker covers — relevant for future substitution sites (notably L5 break-substitution). `tools/ts2pant/package.json` does not currently depend on `fast-check`; property-based testing is new to this codebase.

operationalConsiderations.externalSystemAccess: Not applicable. This gameplan only modifies the ts2pant TypeScript translator (a build-time tool). No new external system is reached. `fast-check` is an npm devDependency for unit tests; npm handles its installation through the existing `package.json` mechanism.
operationalConsiderations.crossRuntimeContracts: Not applicable. The primitive is internal to ts2pant. The migration preserves the existing TS-side ↔ wasm-side contract via `ast.substituteBinder` for Var-by-name substitution on lowered OpaqueExpr; that boundary is unchanged. The TS-side IR1 substitution boundary is internal to ts2pant.
operationalConsiderations.failureBehavior: Two deliberate failure paths. (a) Capture risk: when the replacement's free vars would be captured by a haystack binder, the primitive throws `CaptureRiskError` synchronously. This is a programmer-error signal — callers are expected to allocate replacements fresh against scope before invoking. No existing caller hits this path (both migrate cleanly with snapshot-equivalence). (b) Unrecognised needle: the primitive accepts only Var and Member needles; other kinds throw `Error("unsupported needle kind")`. Both errors surface via JavaScript's standard exception mechanism; callers do not catch (these are bug indicators, not recoverable conditions). No retry semantics, no timeout, no I/O.
operationalConsiderations.concurrencyAndIdempotency: Not applicable. The primitive is a pure function over IR1 ADT values; ts2pant is single-threaded and processes one source file at a time. No shared state, no race conditions. Multiple invocations on the same inputs return structurally identical outputs.
operationalConsiderations.rollbackStrategy: Forward-only. The gameplan is additive (new module, new tests, new devDep, new docs) with two file deletions (the old walkers, after their callers migrate). `git revert` of the gameplan's PRs in reverse order restores the hand-rolled walkers and removes the new module. Snapshot tests gate every step; any drift aborts the gameplan via PR-level review before merge.

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flag. The gameplan introduces a new module with no production behavior change (the primitive is dormant after Patch 3; the migrations are snapshot-equivalent in Patches 4 and 5). Toggling at runtime serves no purpose — either the migration is correct (snapshot tests pass) or it is wrong (snapshot tests fail and the gameplan aborts). Every patch is INFRA.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Sequential with one parallel pair. Patch 1 adds the `fast-check` devDep (mechanical). Patch 2 lands pending unit + property test stubs naming Patch 3. Patch 3 implements the primitive and un-skips the tests; this is the substantive correctness patch. Patches 4 and 5 are independent migrations (different files) that run in parallel; both depend on Patch 3. Patch 6 lands documentation after both migrations complete.

requiredChanges.description: Add `fast-check` to `devDependencies`. Run `npm install` to update `package-lock.json`.

Create the test file with hand-written unit-test stubs (skipped, with PENDING Patch 3 comments) covering each binder-site and shadowing case, plus a `describe('properties', ...)` block with fast-check property-test stubs.

New module exporting `freeVarsIR1Expr`, `freeVarsIR1Stmt`, `substituteIR1ExprSubtree`, `substituteIR1StmtSubtree`, and a `CaptureRiskError` class. Enumerates IR1 binder sites exhaustively: `IR1Expr.each` binder, `IR1Stmt.let` name, `IR1Stmt.foreach` binder, `IR1Stmt.for` init's let-counter. Throws `CaptureRiskError` when the replacement's free vars would be captured by a haystack binder.
requiredChanges.signature:
export class CaptureRiskError extends Error { constructor(binderName: string, capturedVar: string) }
export function freeVarsIR1Expr(expr: IR1Expr): Set<string>
export function freeVarsIR1Stmt(stmt: IR1Stmt): Set<string>
export function substituteIR1ExprSubtree(haystack: IR1Expr, needle: IR1Expr, replacement: IR1Expr): IR1Expr
export function substituteIR1StmtSubtree(haystack: IR1Stmt, needle: IR1Expr, replacement: IR1Expr): IR1Stmt

requiredChanges.description: Delete `substituteL1Subtree` and `structuralEqualL1` (if only used by `substituteL1Subtree`). The functor-lift recognizer's call sites use `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` from `ir1-substitute.ts` instead.
Delete `substituteMemberReads`. The fixed-point lowerer's call sites use `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` from `ir1-substitute.ts` instead.

Add a discipline statement under § "SSA Foundations" or in a new sibling subsection: "All IR1-level substitution goes through `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` / `substituteIR1StmtSubtree` in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`. Hand-rolled walkers in this layer are forbidden. New IR1 binder forms must extend the primitive's binder-site enumeration in the same patch that introduces them."

Extend the PR #84 Post-Mortem section with a sibling entry citing PR #226 as the IR1-layer instance of the same lesson: per-site hand-rolled substitution walkers recreate the same hygiene questions. The fix is one centralised primitive; the discipline is documented in the IR doc.

contextResources.why: Authoritative source for the gameplan's Definition of Done, the locked decisions (TS-side primitive; named-binder rep; throw on capture risk; separate Expr/Stmt entry points; snapshot-equivalent migrations), and the established precedents. Every patch's implementing agent reads this for context on the workstream's scope and constraints.

`substituteL1Subtree` (line 1748 onward) is the predecessor walker that Patch 4 migrates and deletes. The migration must preserve its observable behavior on functor-lift fixtures. The walker has partial binder-scope handling at the `each` case (Var-needle shadowing only); the new primitive supersedes this with exhaustive binder-site handling plus capture-risk rejection. `structuralEqualL1` may also be deletable if only used by `substituteL1Subtree`; the implementing agent checks call sites.

`substituteMemberReads` (line 358 onward) is the predecessor walker that Patch 5 migrates and deletes. The migration must preserve its observable behavior on fixed-point fixtures. The walker has no binder-scope handling at all — it recurses into `each` body unconditionally. Its current correctness relies on the fixed-point lowerer's caller-side fresh-binder allocation (PR #226 confirmed addressing the capture risk via fresh-name allocation against guard/body scope). The new primitive's exhaustive binder handling subsumes this.

The IR1 ADT defines every form the primitive's walker must traverse. Patch 3 must enumerate every binder-introducing form exhaustively: `IR1Expr.each` (binder field), `IR1Stmt.let` (name field; scope is subsequent block statements), `IR1Stmt.foreach` (binder field; scope is body and fold leaves), `IR1Stmt.for` (init's let if present; scope is cond/step/body). Missing one is a capture bug. The implementing agent reads this file to verify exhaustiveness against the current ADT. Patch 2 also consults this to design property-test generators that produce realistic IR1 trees with random binder structure.

Snapshot equivalence is the migration gate. Patches 4 and 5 must produce byte-identical Pant output on every fixture currently exercising the walkers they migrate. The functor-lift fixtures exercise `substituteL1Subtree`; the fixed-point fixture exercises `substituteMemberReads`. Any snapshot drift aborts the gameplan; the implementing agent must not regenerate snapshots — a regeneration request is a stop-the-line condition that requires the migration logic to be re-examined.

Property-based testing library for TS/JS used by Patch 2's property-test stubs and Patch 3's implementation tests. Patch 2 imports `fc` from fast-check; designs `Arbitrary<IR1Expr>` and `Arbitrary<IR1Stmt>` generators with random binder structure. Patch 3 implements the properties: free-vars-after-substitution composition, idempotence on no-occurrence, identity under shadowing binder, no capture leakage. fast-check's `fc.letrec` is the natural API for recursive ADT generators.

Keep bound and free variable names disjoint; substitution cannot capture if the convention holds. The primitive enforces a relaxed form: the replacement must be closed against haystack binders (no free var in replacement matches any binder in scope); violations throw `CaptureRiskError`. The convention is the foundational invariant Patch 3's capture-check encodes.

Term Rewriting and All That, Chapter 2: positions, subterms, substitution. The primitive's structural recursion follows this discipline — substitution is a partial function over term positions; binder scope partitions positions into 'open under this binder' and 'closed'. Already cited in `tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md` for the functor-lift's L1 operand rewriting; this gameplan generalises the same technique.

The target document for Patch 6's discipline statement. The new statement sits alongside § SSA Foundations or as a sibling subsection. The wording style should match the existing IR doc's framing (precedent references, file paths to the primitive module, the forbidden-walker statement).

The PR #84 Post-Mortem section is the template for Patch 6's sibling entry citing PR #226. The PR #84 entry frames a hand-rolled substitution bug as a class of errors that standard algorithms prevent; this gameplan's entry cites the IR1-layer instance of the same lesson. Same five-bug-categories table style if applicable, or a prose addition if the IR1 case doesn't decompose into the same shape.

acceptanceCriteria: The primitive throws `CaptureRiskError` synchronously when the replacement's free vars would be captured by a haystack binder.
The primitive halts at a binder that shadows a Var needle (substitution does not descend into the binder's scope).
Hand-written unit tests cover each IR1 binder site (`each`, `let`, `foreach`, `for` init) and the shadowing / capture-risk paths.

Property-based tests via `fast-check` confirm: (a) free vars after substitution = (FV(h) \ {needle if Var}) ∪ FV(r) on no-capture inputs; (b) substitution is idempotent when needle does not occur; (c) substitution under a shadowing binder is identity; (d) capture-risk inputs throw.
`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-build.ts` no longer defines `substituteL1Subtree`; the functor-lift recognizer calls `substituteIR1ExprSubtree`.
`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` no longer defines `substituteMemberReads`; the fixed-point lowerer calls `substituteIR1ExprSubtree`.
`tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot` is byte-identical before and after the migration; any drift aborts the gameplan.
`tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md` documents the discipline that all IR1 substitution goes through the new primitive.
`tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md`'s PR #84 Post-Mortem section gains a sibling entry citing PR #226.
Full ts2pant test suite passes (unit + integration), including the new property-based tests and all existing fixtures.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Throw `CaptureRiskError` synchronously when the replacement's free vars would be captured by a haystack binder, rather than returning a tagged result or alpha-renaming automatically.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Standard JavaScript idiom for programmer-error conditions — the caller's contract is 'ensure replacement is closed against haystack binders before calling.' Symmetric with how Bindlib avoids the question entirely by alpha-renaming on the OCaml side; the TS-side primitive can't alpha-rename without pulling NameRegistry into a pure module, so 'reject loudly' is the simpler invariant. Tests use `assert.throws(...)` to verify the rejection path. Future workstreams that need alpha-renaming can extend the primitive then; the locked-decision in the workstream defers this.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Separate `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` and `substituteIR1StmtSubtree` entry points sharing a common dispatch helper, rather than one polymorphic entry.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Type-safe at the call site; callers know which signature they're invoking. Matches existing ts2pant convention (e.g., `lowerL1Expr` vs `lowerL1Stmt`, `freeVarsIR1Expr` vs `freeVarsIR1Stmt`). One polymorphic entry would force every caller to narrow on the result type via cast or shape check — adds ergonomic friction with no real benefit.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Statement-level substitution is in scope for this milestone, not deferred.
explicitOpinions.rationale: The workstream's M1 DoD explicitly names both. Statement-level adds modest complexity (block-scope binder threading for `let` / `foreach` / `for` init) but matches the contract L5 break-substitution will need. Deferring statement support would leave L5 with a third hand-rolled walker risk and require re-opening the gameplan; doing it once now is correct.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Property tests and unit tests live in a single file `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts` with separate `describe` blocks, rather than separate files.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Matches existing ts2pant test conventions — one file per module under test. The property-test block uses `describe('properties', ...)` to group fast-check assertions; unit tests use `describe('unit', ...)`. Easy to navigate; one place to look when reasoning about substitution correctness. Splitting would add a file with no real organisational benefit.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Migrations land as two independent patches (one per migrated site), running in parallel via the dependency graph, rather than a single bundled migration patch.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Each migration is a focused review concern: Patch 4 verifies functor-lift behavior on `expressions-functor-lift*.ts` snapshots; Patch 5 verifies fixed-point behavior on `functions-mutating-fixed-point-while.ts` snapshots. Reviewers can focus on one call site's semantic preservation at a time. The orchestrator parallelises them since they touch different files. Bundling would not save review effort and would make a single PR larger than necessary.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Single milestone / single gameplan, not two milestones (primitive + migration).
explicitOpinions.rationale: An earlier draft of the workstream split this into M1 (dormant primitive) and M2 (migration). On re-examination against the atomicity criteria, no human intervention, observation window, or outcome-conditional decision sits between the primitive landing and the migration starting. The migration is mechanical given the new API; snapshot tests catch any primitive correctness issue regardless of milestone boundary. The split was self-fulfilling ('two milestones admits a safe pause' → but no pause was needed). One atomic gameplan with `dependsOn` between patches is the right structure.

patches.title: Pending unit and property tests for IR1 substitution primitive

files.description: New test file with hand-written unit-test stubs (skipped, with `// PENDING Patch 3:` comments) and a `describe('properties', ...)` block with fast-check property-test stubs.

changes: Create `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts` modelled on existing ts2pant test files (e.g., `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.test.mts`).
Import `fc` from `fast-check`. Import IR1 constructors from `../src/ir1.js`. Do NOT import from `../src/ir1-substitute.js` — that module does not exist yet.

Under `describe('unit', () => { ... })`, add `it.skip(...)` stubs for: `freeVarsIR1Expr` on var/lit/each; `freeVarsIR1Stmt` on block/let/foreach/for; `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` replacing leaf Var; replacing Member; halting at shadowing each binder; throwing CaptureRiskError on capture risk; preserving structure outside replacement; `substituteIR1StmtSubtree` threading block-scope binders; handling foreach binder; handling for-init let.

Under `describe('properties', () => { ... })`, add `it.skip(...)` stubs for: free-vars composition (FV(substitute(h, n, r)) = (FV(h) \ {n if Var}) ∪ FV(r)) under no-capture inputs; idempotence on no-occurrence; identity under shadowing binder; capture-risk inputs throw. Each stub designs the fast-check generator skeleton in a comment but does not execute (skipped).
Each stub body contains only the PENDING comment naming Patch 3 — no references to symbols that don't exist yet.
Run `npx tsx --test tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts` to confirm the file compiles and stubs are reported as skipped.

precedents.name: fast-check
precedents.whyApplicable: Property-based testing library for TS/JS. Patch 2's property-test stubs use `fc.letrec` for recursive ADT generators (IR1Expr / IR1Stmt) and `fc.assert(fc.property(gen, prop))` for the assertion API. The stub bodies design the generator skeletons inline (as comments) without running them; Patch 3 finalises and un-skips them.

patches.title: Implement IR1 substitution primitive; un-skip tests

files.description: New module exporting `CaptureRiskError`, `freeVarsIR1Expr`, `freeVarsIR1Stmt`, `substituteIR1ExprSubtree`, `substituteIR1StmtSubtree`. Walks every IR1Expr and IR1Stmt form. Threads in-scope binders; throws `CaptureRiskError` on capture risk; halts at Var-needle shadowing binder; recurses structurally otherwise.

Remove `.skip` from every `it.skip(...)` introduced in Patch 2. Replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions. Property tests use `fc.assert(fc.property(gen, prop), { numRuns: 100 })` (default) or higher for stress.

changes: Create `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`. Import IR1 types and constructors from `./ir1.js`.

Export `class CaptureRiskError extends Error { binderName: string; capturedVar: string; constructor(binderName, capturedVar) { super(`IR1 substitution would capture free var '${capturedVar}' under binder '${binderName}'`); this.binderName = binderName; this.capturedVar = capturedVar; } }`.

Implement `freeVarsIR1Expr(expr: IR1Expr): Set<string>`. Recurse over every IR1Expr kind. For `var`, return `{name}`. For binder-introducing forms (`each`), return `freeVars(children) \ {binder}`. For non-binder forms, return the union of children's free vars.

Implement `freeVarsIR1Stmt(stmt: IR1Stmt): Set<string>`. Recurse over every IR1Stmt kind. For `block`, fold over statements left-to-right, accumulating free vars and removing names introduced by `let` statements as scope is extended. For `foreach`, return `freeVars(source) ∪ (freeVars(body) \ {binder})`. For `for`, accumulate similarly through init/cond/step/body. For `let`, return `freeVars(value)` (the name is in scope only for SUBSEQUENT statements, not for the let's own value).

Implement `substituteIR1ExprSubtree(haystack, needle, replacement)`. Compute `freeVarsIR1Expr(replacement)` once. Recurse over haystack: (a) if `haystack` structurally equals `needle`, return `replacement`; (b) at each binder-introducing form, check `freeVarsOfReplacement ∩ {binder} ≠ ∅` → throw `CaptureRiskError(binder, captured)`; check if `needle.kind === 'var' && needle.name === binder` → halt (return haystack unchanged); otherwise recurse into children with binder added to scope context; (c) for non-binder forms, recurse into children and reconstruct.

Implement `substituteIR1StmtSubtree(haystack, needle, replacement)`. Same recursion discipline; threads block-scope binders via a `scopedBinders: Set<string>` parameter that grows as `let` statements are traversed within a block.
Restrict `needle` to `kind: 'var'` or `kind: 'member'`. Other kinds throw `Error('unsupported needle kind: ...')`.

Structural-equality helper for needle matching: a private `ir1ExprStructurallyEqual(a, b): boolean` that recursively compares Var and Member shapes (mirrors the existing `structuralEqualL1` in `ir1-build.ts` — read that for reference, but re-implement here so the module is self-contained).

In `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts`, remove `.skip` from every stub from Patch 2. Replace PENDING-comment bodies with concrete assertions. Unit tests build hand-coded IR1 trees via the `ir1*` constructors and call the primitive directly. Property tests build fc.Arbitrary generators using `fc.letrec`.

Property test generators: design `arbIR1Expr` and `arbIR1Stmt` with bounded depth (≤4) and a curated set of var names ('a' through 'f'). Random binders pulled from the same name set so collisions occur naturally. The generators do NOT need full IR1 coverage — they need binder-rich shapes that exercise the scope logic.
Add a `.gitignore` entry or biome config exclusion if fast-check's run-time output pollutes CI logs (unlikely; check after first run).
Run `npx tsx --test tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts` and confirm all tests pass.

precedents.name: Barendregt convention
precedents.whyApplicable: The primitive enforces a relaxed form of the Barendregt convention: replacement's free vars must not intersect haystack binders. Violations throw `CaptureRiskError`. This is the foundational invariant; the capture-check is one line of set intersection per binder encountered during the recursion.

precedents.name: Baader & Nipkow, Term Rewriting and All That, Ch. 2
precedents.whyApplicable: Positions, subterms, substitution. The walker's structural recursion follows the textbook discipline: identify the position (subterm matches needle?), substitute or recurse, respecting binder scope as a position-partitioning device. Read Ch. 2 if uncertain about edge cases like needle ≡ haystack (return replacement directly without descent) or empty subtree handling.

precedents.name: fast-check
precedents.whyApplicable: Property tests use `fc.letrec` to build recursive `Arbitrary<IR1Expr>` and `Arbitrary<IR1Stmt>` generators. The properties (free-vars composition, idempotence, shadowing identity, capture rejection) are encoded as `fc.assert(fc.property(...))`. `fc.commands` is overkill for this primitive; plain property assertions suffice. Default `numRuns: 100` is fine; bump per property if specific bugs slip through.

ir1-substitute > unit > freeVarsIR1Expr respects each binder
ir1-substitute > unit > freeVarsIR1Stmt respects let / foreach / for binders
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree replaces a leaf Var
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree replaces a Member subtree
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree halts at a shadowing each binder
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree throws CaptureRiskError on capture
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree preserves shape outside replacement sites
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree threads block-scope let binders
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree handles foreach binder scope
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree handles for-init let scope
ir1-substitute > properties > free vars compose under substitution
ir1-substitute > properties > substitution is idempotent when needle does not occur
ir1-substitute > properties > substitution under shadowing binder is identity

module TS2PANT_IR1_SUBSTITUTION_PATCH_3.

> Patch 3 implements the capture-avoiding substitution
> primitive. Replacement's free vars must not collide with
> haystack binders; violations throw CaptureRiskError.
> Var needles whose names match a binder halt at the binder
> boundary (substitution does not descend into shadowed
> scope). Non-binder forms recurse structurally.

IR1Expr.
IR1Stmt.
Name.
FreeVarSet.
SubResult.
NeedleKind.

member => NeedleKind.

binders-of-expr e: IR1Expr => FreeVarSet.
binders-of-stmt s: IR1Stmt => FreeVarSet.

---

> Capture-risk detection: replacement free vars intersecting
> haystack binders cause a throw.
all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr |
  capture-risk? (binders-of-expr h) (free-vars-expr r) <-> throws-capture-error? h n r.

> Clean substitution returns a result with composed free vars
> (the no-capture path).
all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr |
  substitutes-cleanly? h n r -> ~(throws-capture-error? h n r).

> A Var needle whose name matches a binder halts at that binder
> (substitution under shadow is identity for that subtree).
all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr, b: Name |
  needle-kind n = var and shadows-needle? b n ->
    is-identity-under-shadow? h n r.

patches.title: Migrate functor-lift's substituteL1Subtree to the primitive

files.description: Delete `substituteL1Subtree` (and `structuralEqualL1` if only used by it). Replace its call sites in the functor-lift recognizer with `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` from `./ir1-substitute.js`.

Spot-check that existing functor-lift unit tests still pass. Add at most one new test if a useful additional case surfaces during migration (e.g., a previously-untested capture-risk scenario that now throws); not required, the snapshot suite is the load-bearing signal.

Must be byte-identical after the migration. If `npm run test:update-snapshots` produces a diff for any functor-lift fixture (`expressions-functor-lift.ts`, `expressions-functor-lift-property.ts`), the migration has changed behavior and is a STOP — do not commit the diff; abort the patch.

changes: In `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-build.ts`, identify the call site(s) of `substituteL1Subtree` (likely in `tryRecognizeFunctorLift` or a sibling helper). Note: the existing walker returns `{result, changed}`; the new primitive returns `IR1Expr` directly. Callers need to track the 'changed' flag separately if they depend on it (verify by reading caller code).

Replace each call site with `substituteIR1ExprSubtree(haystack, needle, replacement)`. If the caller needed `changed`, derive it from `result !== haystack` (reference equality — the primitive returns the original haystack unchanged when no substitution occurs).
Delete the `substituteL1Subtree` function definition (around line 1748).
Delete `structuralEqualL1` if it has no other callers. Grep `structuralEqualL1` across `tools/ts2pant/src/` to confirm before deletion. If other callers exist, leave it.

Run `just ts2pant-test` to confirm zero regression. If unit tests fail because the new primitive throws CaptureRiskError on a case the old walker silently allowed, the caller is allocating a replacement with a free var that matches a haystack binder — that's a latent bug the new primitive surfaced. Investigate and fix at the call site (allocate a fresher replacement name); do NOT relax the primitive's capture check.
Run `just ts2pant-test-integration` to confirm functor-lift `pant --check` integration tests pass.
Inspect the constructs snapshot diff: `git diff tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot`. Expect empty diff. If non-empty, abort and investigate.

patches.title: Migrate fixed-point's substituteMemberReads to the primitive

files.description: Delete `substituteMemberReads`. Replace its call sites in the fixed-point lowerer with `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` from `./ir1-substitute.js`.
Spot-check that existing fixed-point unit tests still pass. The user-`s` collision regression test added in PR #226 should still pass via the new primitive.

Must be byte-identical after the migration. If `npm run test:update-snapshots` produces a diff for `functions-mutating-fixed-point-while.ts`, the migration has changed behavior and is a STOP — do not commit the diff; abort the patch.

changes: In `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`, identify the call sites of `substituteMemberReads` (line 128, 158, and any others). They likely look like `substituteMemberReads(expr, member, stateVar)`.
Replace each call site with `substituteIR1ExprSubtree(expr, member, stateVar)`. The signature matches.
Delete the `substituteMemberReads` function definition (around line 358 onward, ~80 lines of recursive switch).

Run `just ts2pant-test` to confirm zero regression. The user-`s` collision regression test (added in PR #226) verifies the primitive's capture-check correctly fires when the loop body contains a `s` variable shadowing the state binder — the new primitive should pass this with the same fresh-binder allocation logic the caller already does.
Run `just ts2pant-test-integration` to confirm fixed-point `pant --check` integration tests pass.
Inspect the constructs snapshot diff: `git diff tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot`. Expect empty diff. If non-empty, abort and investigate.

patches.title: Document the IR1 substitution discipline

files.description: Add a subsection (sibling to § "SSA Foundations") stating the discipline: all IR1 substitution goes through the new primitive; hand-rolled walkers are forbidden; new IR1 binder forms must extend the primitive's binder-site enumeration in the patch that introduces them.

Extend the PR #84 Post-Mortem section with a sibling entry citing PR #226 as the IR1-layer instance of the same lesson — per-site hand-rolled substitution walkers recreate the same hygiene questions; one centralised primitive resolves them.

changes: In `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md`, add a new subsection (e.g., `## IR1 Substitution Discipline`) immediately after `## SSA Foundations` or as part of the existing layering principle. Body: "All IR1-level substitution goes through `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` / `substituteIR1StmtSubtree` in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`. Hand-rolled walkers in this layer are forbidden — review will reject any IR1 substitution that does not use the primitive. When a future patch introduces a new IR1 binder form, the same patch must extend the primitive's binder-site enumeration so capture-checking remains exhaustive. The OCaml-backed `ast.substituteBinder` covers the L3 (lowered OpaqueExpr) Var-by-name case; the new TS-side primitive covers IR1's Var and Member subtree cases. Together they enforce capture-avoidance discipline across every substitution surface in ts2pant."

In `tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md`'s `## PR #84 Post-Mortem` section, add a closing paragraph or sibling block: "**PR #226 (IR1-layer instance, 2026-05).** Per-site hand-rolled IR1 substitution walkers (`substituteL1Subtree` in `ir1-build.ts`; `substituteMemberReads` in `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`) each carried the same capture-avoidance discipline as separate per-site code, surfaced for review repeatedly. The fix mirrors PR #84's: extract one centralised primitive (`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`) and delete the per-site walkers. Discipline documented in `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md`. Standard algorithm: capture-avoiding substitution with binder-site enumeration (Barendregt convention; Baader & Nipkow Ch. 2). Future IR1 substitution sites consume the primitive instead of rolling their own."

Confirm the additions read clearly to a fresh reader: someone landing on the IR doc must see the discipline statement before reading the L1 / L2 sections; the AGENTS.md addition must reference the IR doc as the canonical location for the discipline.

testMap.testName: ir1-substitute > unit > freeVarsIR1Expr returns vars from a leaf var
ir1-substitute > unit > freeVarsIR1Expr respects each binder
ir1-substitute > unit > freeVarsIR1Stmt respects let / foreach / for binders
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree replaces a leaf Var
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree replaces a Member subtree
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree halts at a shadowing each binder
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree throws CaptureRiskError on capture
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree preserves shape outside replacement sites
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree threads block-scope let binders
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree handles foreach binder scope
ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1StmtSubtree handles for-init let scope
ir1-substitute > properties > free vars compose under substitution
ir1-substitute > properties > substitution is idempotent when needle does not occur
ir1-substitute > properties > substitution under shadowing binder is identity

mergabilityInsight: All 6 patches are INFRA. Patch 1 adds the fast-check devDep. Patch 2 lands pending tests. Patch 3 implements the primitive and un-skips tests — the substantive correctness patch. Patches 4 and 5 are independent migrations (different files) that run in parallel; both are snapshot-equivalent, gating any drift as a stop-the-line condition. Patch 6 lands documentation. No BEHAVIOR patches because the migrations preserve observable behavior byte-for-byte; the new primitive's stricter capture-rejection path is never reached by existing callers.
finalStateSpec:
module TS2PANT_IR1_SUBSTITUTION.

> ════════════════════════════════
> Single-milestone workstream: IR1 capture-avoiding substitution.
> ════════════════════════════════
> After this gameplan, ts2pant has one TS-side capture-avoiding
> substitution primitive that handles every IR1-level
> substitution need. The two existing hand-rolled walkers
> (substituteL1Subtree for functor-lift; substituteMemberReads
> for fixed-point) are deleted; their callers route through
> the new primitive. The primitive throws CaptureRiskError on
> capture risk, halts at Var-needle shadowing binders, and
> recurses structurally otherwise. Documentation in the IR doc
> and AGENTS.md PR #84 post-mortem establishes the discipline.

IR1Expr.
IR1Stmt.
Name.
FreeVarSet.
Walker.
Fixture.
SnapshotState.
Document.
DocumentKind.
DocumentMention.
NeedleKind.
Package.
Dependency.
substituteIR1ExprSubtree => Walker.
substituteIR1StmtSubtree => Walker.
substituteL1Subtree => Walker.
substituteMemberReads => Walker.
fast-check => Dependency.
identical => SnapshotState.
drifted => SnapshotState.
ir-doc => DocumentKind.
agents-md => DocumentKind.
substitution-discipline => DocumentMention.
pr-226-postmortem => DocumentMention.
var => NeedleKind.
member => NeedleKind.

has-dev-dependency? p: Package, d: Dependency => Bool.
walker-exists? w: Walker => Bool.
functor-lift-uses w: Walker => Bool.
fixed-point-uses w: Walker => Bool.
needle-kind n: IR1Expr => NeedleKind.
free-vars-expr e: IR1Expr => FreeVarSet.
free-vars-stmt s: IR1Stmt => FreeVarSet.
binders-of-expr e: IR1Expr => FreeVarSet.
binders-of-stmt s: IR1Stmt => FreeVarSet.
capture-risk? haystack-binders: FreeVarSet, repl-free: FreeVarSet => Bool.
substitutes-cleanly? h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr => Bool.
throws-capture-error? h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr => Bool.
is-identity-under-shadow? h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr => Bool.
shadows-needle? binder: Name, n: IR1Expr => Bool.
snapshot-state f: Fixture => SnapshotState.
is-functor-lift-fixture? f: Fixture => Bool.
is-fixed-point-fixture? f: Fixture => Bool.
document-kind d: Document => DocumentKind.
document-mentions? d: Document, m: DocumentMention => Bool.
---

> Dependency.
all p: Package | has-dev-dependency? p fast-check.

> The new primitives exist; the old walkers do not.
walker-exists? substituteIR1ExprSubtree.
walker-exists? substituteIR1StmtSubtree.
~(walker-exists? substituteL1Subtree).
~(walker-exists? substituteMemberReads).

> Both former call sites route through the new primitive.
functor-lift-uses substituteIR1ExprSubtree.
fixed-point-uses substituteIR1ExprSubtree.

> Primitive contract: capture risk throws; clean substitution
> returns the result; shadowing binders halt the descent for
> Var needles.
all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr |
  capture-risk? (binders-of-expr h) (free-vars-expr r) <-> throws-capture-error? h n r.

all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr |
  substitutes-cleanly? h n r -> ~(throws-capture-error? h n r).

all h: IR1Expr, n: IR1Expr, r: IR1Expr, b: Name |
  needle-kind n = var and shadows-needle? b n ->
    is-identity-under-shadow? h n r.

> Snapshot equivalence on every migrated fixture.
all f: Fixture | is-functor-lift-fixture? f -> snapshot-state f = identical.
all f: Fixture | is-fixed-point-fixture? f -> snapshot-state f = identical.

> Documentation invariants.
all d: Document |
  document-kind d = ir-doc ->
    document-mentions? d substitution-discipline.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = agents-md ->
    document-mentions? d pr-226-postmortem.
